summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host, network, and environment data on install
details: tempo-layout@99.0.0 is a dependency-confusion squat (version 99.0.0 chosen to win semver resolution over an internal package) whose preinstall lifecycle hook performs system reconnaissance and exfiltration on every installer. poc.js executes `ipconfig /all` on Windows or `ip a` plus `cat /etc/resolv.conf` on Linux, then HTTPS-POSTs the installer's hostname, username, install directory, platform, network interface details, full environment variable key list, and all `npm_*` environment values to the hardcoded out-of-band interaction host `d8a56vpon5budaeafq00tsyj88aqd5m7p.oast.pro`, with an additional DNS callback to the same domain. index.js prints a warning indicating the target is an internal `@livingdesign/react` package. Although the package self-describes as authorized security research, it is publicly installable on npm and harms any third party that pulls it; the exfiltration runs without consent at `npm install` time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
